legal.updatedAtSite: Last updated: {date}. This notice describes how personal data is processed on {site}.

s1.title: 1) Data Controller
s1.body: The data controller is the owner of the site and the WorkFlow application (a personal tool used by a single technician). For privacy requests you can write to {email}.

items.account: Account data: email and authentication identifiers (managed by Supabase Auth).
items.operational: Operational data entered by the user: clients, interventions/activities, notes, checklists, spare parts, tickets, documents, photos and voice notes uploaded by the user.
items.technical: Technical data: essential error logs and operational information (e.g. sync outcomes), required to keep the app stable.
items.cookies: Cookies / similar technologies: only technical elements that are necessary (e.g. session/auth cookies). No profiling cookies or third‑party analytics are used.

items.auth: Provide access to the app and maintain an authenticated session.
items.manage: Enable management of interventions/activities, clients, documents and spare parts.
items.sync: Synchronise data between devices and cloud when connectivity is available.
items.email: Send technical emails at the user’s request (e.g. sending documents to support), if configured.
items.security: Prevent abuse and ensure service security and integrity.

items.contract: Performance of a service (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR): authentication and core features.
items.legitimate: Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR): security, abuse prevention, stability and debugging.
items.consent: Consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) only where required (e.g. browser notifications when enabled by the user).

s5.title: 5) Processing and retention
s5.p1: WorkFlow is designed as an offline‑first PWA: a copy of data is stored locally on the device (IndexedDB) to allow usage without a network. When online, data can be synchronised to a Supabase backend (database and storage) for multi‑device continuity.
s5.p2: Data is retained for as long as necessary for the purposes above or until a deletion request. As a personal tool, no profiling is performed and data is not sold or shared for commercial purposes.

s6.title: 6) Recipients / processors
s6.p1: Data may be processed via essential technical providers:

items.supabase: Supabase (Auth, Database, Storage, Realtime) as cloud infrastructure.
items.resend: Resend (if configured) for sending emails requested by the user.

s6.p2: Data is not sold. No advertising or third‑party analytics tools are used.

s7.title: 7) Transfers outside the EU/EEA
s7.body: Some providers may process data in countries outside the EEA. Where applicable, transfers are carried out with GDPR safeguards (e.g. Standard Contractual Clauses).

s8.title: 8) Data subject rights
s8.p1: You have the right to obtain: access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, objection, and to lodge a complaint with the Italian Data Protection Authority. To exercise your rights you can:

items.settingsRights: Use the “Your rights” section in Settings (export and data deletion).

s9.title: 9) Minors
s9.body: WorkFlow is not intended for minors and is not designed to intentionally collect data relating to minors.

s1.title: 1) Nature of the service
s1.body: WorkFlow is a personal tool designed for use by a single technician. The site is publicly accessible, but actual use is intended for the owner. It is not a commercial SaaS service with an SLA, guaranteed support or enterprise features.

s2.title: 2) Account and authentication
s2.body: Access is provided via authentication (Supabase). You are responsible for keeping credentials and devices secure. In case of unauthorised use, contact the controller.

items.allowed: Use the app to manage data and workflows related to technical interventions.
items.security: Do not attempt to compromise security, bypass protections or access unauthorised data.
items.illegal: Do not use the service for unlawful activities or to upload content infringing third‑party rights.

s4.title: 4) Uploaded content and data
s4.body: Data you enter (clients, interventions, documents, photos, voice notes, etc.) remains your responsibility. We recommend uploading only what is necessary and avoiding excessive data, especially special categories of data (Art. 9 GDPR) unless strictly necessary.

s5.title: 5) Availability and changes
s5.body: The service may be changed, suspended or discontinued at any time. Temporary outages may occur (maintenance, network or provider issues). As a personal tool, operational continuity is not guaranteed.

s6.title: 6) Limitation of liability
s6.body: To the extent permitted by law, the controller is not liable for indirect or consequential damages, data loss, loss of profits or business interruptions arising from use or inability to use WorkFlow. Use the export/backup features regularly.

s7.title: 7) Intellectual property and licence
s7.body: The software and interface are protected by applicable laws. Use is granted for the controller’s personal/operational purposes. Unauthorised reproduction or distribution is not permitted.

s8.title: 8) Privacy
s8.body: Personal data processing is governed by the Privacy Policy.

s9.title: 9) Contact
s9.body: For communications related to these Terms: {email}.

s1.title: Conformance status
s1.body: WorkFlow is a personal progressive web application (PWA). The goal is to provide an accessible experience aligned with WCAG 2.1 AA principles, within the realistic limits of a personal project.

items.focus: UI components with visible focus and keyboard navigation (Radix/shadcn patterns).
items.contrast: Good contrast in light/dark themes and text that is not overly small.
items.labels: Explicit labels for key controls and regions/navigation with aria-labels where appropriate.
items.semantic: Legal content rendered with semantic HTML (headings, sections, lists).

items.dnd: Some complex screens (e.g. drag & drop editors) may require additional ARIA refinements.
items.pdf: Exported PDFs are generated from an HTML view and may not be fully accessible to screen readers.
items.maps: Map-based features may be less usable without visual support.

s4.title: Feedback and contact
s4.body: If you find an accessibility issue, describe the page and expected behaviour. It will be evaluated and fixed when feasible as the app evolves.

landing.metaDescription: Offline-first PWA for technicians: interventions, documents, reminders, multi-device sync and PDF reports. Data stays on-device and syncs securely when you’re back online.
landing.heroTitle: WorkFlow — your personal assistant for technical visits
landing.heroBody: Plan, document and close field visits even without a connection. WorkFlow keeps data on the device and syncs it safely when you’re back online: less admin friction, more operational control.
landing.value1Title: Built for work on the move
landing.value1Body: Layouts and actions tuned for iPhone and iPad: fewer taps, clearer screens in real working conditions.
landing.value2Body: No blank states when the network drops: write, attach, track time. Sync catches up when you’re online again.
landing.value3Body: PDFs, documents and structured notes feel more reliable than scattered messages and photos.
landing.featuresSubtitle: One tool that ties interventions, light stock, customers and documentation together—without enterprise bloat.
landing.testimonialsSubtitle: Clear goal: less admin overhead, more time on the actual job. Here’s what teams look for—and get—with WorkFlow.
landing.ctaTitle: Ready to get the field under control?
landing.ctaBody: Create an account, install the PWA and start with your first job—no card required to try the core flow.

interventions.title: Interventions and activities
interventions.body: Manage visits, on-site or remote activities, job status, notes and attachments in a flow designed for tablets.

scanner.title: Document scanner
scanner.body: Capture PDFs on site, attach them to a job and send to support when connectivity allows.

reminders.title: Smart reminders
reminders.body: Browser notifications and optional email reminders for due dates—fewer misses, better continuity with customers.

sync.title: Sync across devices
sync.body: IndexedDB locally, Supabase in the cloud: work offline and pick up on your phone, iPad or desktop.

pdf.title: PDF export
pdf.body: Printable reports for customers or internal records, consistent with the job data.

clients.title: Structured clients
clients.body: A proper customer directory with history tied to each visit.

stock.title: Stock and spare parts
stock.body: Track stock movements linked to interventions: what was installed or taken out.

tickets.title: Lightweight CRM tickets
tickets.body: Issues and follow-ups without the overhead of an enterprise CRM—stay quick on the field.

templates.title: Reusable templates
templates.body: Presets for recurring jobs: start pre-filled and save admin time.

reports.title: Reports and stats
reports.body: Exports and operational numbers to understand workload, performance and priorities.

one.quote: The biggest win is closing a job in a basement with no signal, then seeing everything synced when I’m back at the office.

two.quote: Scanning and PDFs replaced messy WhatsApp photo threads and lost paperwork. Customers receive something readable straight away.

three.quote: Due-date reminders on iPad reduced delays on recurring work. The interface is calm and doesn’t get in the way.

protectedShell.offlineFirstNote: Offline-first. Data stays on the device until it syncs.

profile.supportEmailHint: It will be pre-filled in the “To:” field when you send a document. It syncs to your profile when you’re online.
profile.reminderEmailHint: Used when an intervention enables reminders but leaves its own email blank. Requires Resend env on the server.

profile.notificationPermissionBody: You can change this in the browser site settings.
profile.officeAddressHint: Used by the Round trip button (Start → Office → Start) when creating an intervention. Synced to your user profile when online.

sync.title: Cloud sync

toasts.syncFinishedBody: Your data is up to date.

rights.title: Your rights

rights.confirmDeleteAll:
Do you want to delete ALL your data?

- It will be removed from this device.
- If you are online, it will also be deleted from the cloud.

This cannot be undone.
rights.note: Note: if you’re offline, the cloud deletion will be retried when you’re back online (local data is deleted immediately).

route.navigatorHint: On iPad, Apple Maps opens with all stops; on other devices, Google Maps may be used if it’s a better fit.

detail.deleteDialogBody: When you are online, this also removes the intervention from your Supabase account (documents, attachments, and queued emails in the cloud). CRM tickets stay but are unlinked. This cannot be undone.

deleteDialog.title: Delete intervention?
deleteDialog.body: Remove “{label}” from this device and from the cloud when you are online, including linked documents, photos, voice notes, and stock movements for this visit. Tickets are only unlinked. You cannot undo this.

toasts.deleteCloudFailedTitle: Could not delete in the cloud
toasts.deletedQueuedBody: Deleted locally. Will be removed from the cloud when you are online.
